headline: How The Copart (CPRT) Investment Story Is Shifting With New Price Targets And Risks
summary: Price targets on Copart have been reset lower, with JPMorgan moving from US$45 to US$34 and another firm trimming its target from US$33 to US$32, even as the model fair value holds at US$42.67. These changes reflect analyst concerns around fee rate competition, execution risk, and a recent fiscal Q2 miss, while still supporting active coverage and, in JPMorgan’s case, a Neutral stance. Read on to see how to interpret these shifting targets and what to watch as the Copart story develops from...
